content:
stop learning frameworks

start learning how to sell

the money is not in the code

your SaaS doesn't need 50 features

it needs 1 feature and 10 paying users

ship it

"I need a co-founder to start"

no you don't

you need Claude Code and a credit card for Stripe

junior devs mass applying to jobs

senior devs mass prompting AI agents

we are not the same

stop building in silence for 6 months

start building in public for 6 days

the feedback will save you

the best code

is the code you mass delete after launch

because nobody needed that feature

deja de buscar la idea perfecta

agarra algo que te moleste todos los dias

y resolvelo

asi de simple

"primero termino el producto"

no

primero consegui un cliente

despues ves que producto construir

el dev que sabe vender

le gana al dev que sabe 10 frameworks

siempre

sin excepciones

levanto inversion para un MVP

hermano

una landing + Stripe + IA

y validas en un finde

6 meses planeando sin lanzar nada

yo hace 3 anos

no sean como yo

stop building mobile apps

start building desktop apps

trust me

"what tech stack should I use"

the one that lets you ship this week

not the one Twitter told you is cool

your product is not too early to launch

you are too scared to launch it

different problems

built a SaaS in a weekend with AI

spent 3 months trying to get 10 users

the hard part was never the code

el 90% de las features que planeas

nadie las va a usar

lanza con lo minimo

escucha

itera

stop watching tutorials

start building something ugly that works

refactor later

or never

Copilot autocompletes lines

Claude Code refactors entire modules

they are not the same tool

stop comparing them

si tu SaaS no cobra desde el dia 1

no tenes un negocio

tenes un side project con landing page

the best marketing strategy for devs

build in public

that's it

that's the tweet

raised $2M for an app

one dev with Claude Code built the same thing in a weekend

the game changed and VCs didn't notice

you don't need a CS degree

you don't need 5 years of experience

you need a problem worth solving

and the discipline to ship

la deuda tecnica esta sobrevalorada

si nadie usa tu producto

no tenes deuda tecnica

tenes un repo con 0 estrellas

every dev wants to build the next big thing

the devs making money

are building boring invoice tools for dentists

"AI will replace developers"

no it won't

it will replace developers who refuse to use AI

big difference

me canse de escuchar "necesito terminar el producto"

no

necesitas un cliente

el producto se construye despues

stop optimizing for scale

you have 0 users

optimize for getting 1

Cursor for daily coding

Claude Code for architecture

v0 for quick UIs

that's the stack

stop overthinking it

tu primer SaaS no te va a hacer millonario

pero te va a ensenar todo lo que no sabias que no sabias

y eso vale mas que cualquier curso

a mediocre product with great distribution

beats

a great product that nobody knows exists

every single time

I mass deleted 2000 lines of code today

product works better now

less is more is not just a saying

"necesito un equipo para arrancar"

no

necesitas una laptop y cafe

el equipo viene despues de facturar

freemium is a trap for indie hackers

1000 free users eating your support time

0 revenue

just charge from day 1

the dev who mass ships

beats the dev who mass plans

every time

si pudiera volver 3 anos atras

no aprenderia un framework

aprenderia a vender

el framework lo aprendo sobre la marcha

your startup doesn't need

a Notion workspace
a Slack channel
a project manager
a sprint board

it needs customers

hot take:

most "how to build a SaaS" courses

are sold by people who never built a successful SaaS

the only real course is shipping one and failing

Scrum para un equipo de 1 persona

es como usar un camion para ir al kiosco

un Kanban simple y a construir

stop asking "is this idea good enough"

start asking "will someone pay for this today"

if yes ship it

if no next idea

un SaaS con 100 usuarios a USD 50/mes

son USD 5000/mes

no necesitas millones de usuarios

necesitas un nicho con un dolor real

I mass prompt AI all day

and mass ship features all night

this is the indie hacker way

3 months building in silence

vs

1 week building in public

the feedback from 1 week

is worth more than 3 months of assumptions

la soledad del indie hacker

nadie te dice lo dificil que es

construir solo es poderoso

pero necesitas una comunidad

o te volves loco

Next.js + Supabase + Stripe + Claude Code

SaaS funcional en un finde

antes necesitabas un equipo y 3 meses

let that sink in

unpopular opinion:

you don't need to know how to code to launch a SaaS in 2025

you need to know how to think product

and how to use AI

cold email done right

is still the best B2B acquisition channel

but most people send garbage

so they think it doesn't work

los que mas facturan en tech

no son los que mejor programan

son los que mejor entienden problemas de negocio

controversial? puede ser

cierto? tambien

stop chasing VC money

start chasing 10 paying customers

one of these makes you rich

the other makes you an employee with extra steps

every developer has 7 abandoned side projects

the problem is not starting

the problem is finishing

always has been

pricing tip:

your SaaS is too cheap

raise prices

watch what happens

(nothing bad)

di que si a todo cuando empezas

di que no a todo cuando ya factures

el filtro es la plata

Claude Code understands your entire codebase

Codex works in an isolated sandbox

seems like a small detail

it changes everything

the best time to launch your SaaS

was 6 months ago

the second best time

is today

stop waiting

"la IA no sirve para programar"

- alguien que escribio "haceme una app" y se enojo con el resultado

dale contexto y despues me contas

distribution > product

you can have the best product in the world

if nobody finds it

it doesn't exist

le pedi a Claude Code que refactorice un modulo entero

3 minutos

a mano me llevaba 2 horas

y hay gente que todavia piensa que usar IA es trampa

SEO for SaaS is a long game

but when it kicks in

it's a free lead machine that never sleeps

start writing today

analisis paralisis

6 meses pensando

0 lineas de codigo

0 clientes

0 revenue

suena conocido?

a solo dev mass shipping with AI

builds faster than

a team of 5 with daily standups

I will not be taking questions

si tu SaaS depende de una sola fuente de trafico

estas en peligro

Google te cambia el algoritmo manana

y chau negocio

diversifica

the barrier to building a SaaS in 2025

is basically zero

the barrier to getting customers

is still massive

the problem was never technical

micro-SaaS > mega-startup

less risk

positive cashflow fast

no investors breathing down your neck

freedom

el networking en tech

no es ir a eventos y repartir tarjetas

es compartir lo que aprendes

ayudar a otros

y construir relaciones reales online

"what if someone steals my idea"

nobody wants your idea

they can barely execute their own

just build it

construir en publico

me dio mas oportunidades en 1 ano

que 5 anos trabajando en silencio

pero hay que bancarse mostrar los fracasos tambien

your MVP does not need:

dark mode

i18n

microservices

kubernetes

your MVP needs:

1 user who pays

the AI coding tools landscape in 2025:

Claude Code for reasoning
CodEx for async tasks
Cursor for the IDE

there's no winner-takes-all

there's one for each workflow

hot take:

la mayoria de los SaaS no necesitan un CTO

necesitan un fundador con vision de producto

que sepa usar Claude Code

I mass deleted features last week

churn went down

sometimes less product = better product

"necesito saber 5 lenguajes para ser buen dev"

no

necesitas saber pensar bien

dar contexto

y tomar decisiones

el codigo lo escribe la IA

Twitter for B2B SaaS

is a gold mine

but most people post generic content nobody cares about

share your real process

that's 10x more powerful

el churn rate es la metrica mas importante de un SaaS

y nadie habla de eso cuando empieza

todos hablan de MRR

pero si tus usuarios se van

el MRR no sirve

stop debating tabs vs spaces

start debating how to get your first paying customer

that's the only debate that matters

shipped a feature nobody asked for: 2 weeks

shipped a feature users begged for: 2 hours

guess which one moved the needle

la diferencia entre un hobby project y un negocio

es una:

alguien paga

si nadie paga

no importa que tan bueno sea el producto

I mass prompt

I mass ship

I mass iterate

this is the way

vos podes tener el producto mas increible del mundo

pero si nadie lo encuentra

no existe

distribucion > producto

siempre

the tooling for AI coding evolves so fast

what I used 6 months ago is already obsolete

if you're not adapting weekly

you're falling behind

don't compete with big companies on features

compete on speed

on personal attention

on niche

a fast indie hacker beats a slow corp

always

me sorprende la gente que quiere lanzar un SaaS

pero nunca hablo con un potencial cliente

primero vendelo

despues construilo

your CI/CD pipeline has 47 steps

your product has 0 users

priorities

the first $100 in MRR

hits different

it's proof that someone values what you built

enough to pay every month

chase that feeling

el fracaso en startups es baratisimo

lo peor que pasa es que perdes tiempo y aprendes un monton

no entiendo por que la gente le tiene tanto miedo

Claude Code with sub-agents

is like having a dev team that never sleeps

never complains

and never asks for equity

the future is wild

most SaaS die before $1k MRR

not because of the product

but because the founder got bored

persistence is the ultimate feature

pregunta honesta:

cuantos side projects tienen abandonados?

yo tengo 7

el tema no es arrancar

es terminar

you mass mass mass apply to jobs

or

you mass mass mass ship products

choose your grind

Codex is open source

Claude Code has better reasoning

which matters more to you

openness or results

honest debate

hay algo que nadie dice:

construir un SaaS es 20% producto

y 80% distribucion, soporte y operaciones

si solo te gusta la parte tecnica

busca un co-founder de negocio

the dev who mass ships ugly MVPs

learns more in 1 month

than the dev who mass reads documentation for a year

tenes una idea de SaaS?

no la guardes

compartila

te prometo que nadie te la va a robar

y si lo hacen la ejecucion es lo que vale

I mass refactored an entire auth module with Claude Code

4 minutes

first try

no broken interfaces

try doing that manually

the indie hackers I admire most

don't make millions

they make enough to live on their own terms

that's the real flex

me di cuenta que paso mas tiempo decidiendo que construir

que construyendo

y esa es la parte mas importante

la ejecucion la aceleras con IA

la estrategia no

stop mass saving bookmarks about productivity

start mass shipping the thing you've been "planning"

your bookmarks won't build your SaaS

escribir codigo ya no es la habilidad mas valiosa

saber QUE construir

para QUIEN

y como distribuirlo

eso separa a los que facturan de los que no

every time someone spends 6 months planning without launching

I remember myself 3 years ago

analysis paralysis is real

and it's expensive

cada vez que alguien me dice

"mi idea es como Uber pero para..."

me muero un poco por dentro

las buenas ideas de SaaS son aburridas y especificas

your SaaS pricing is probably too cheap

raise it

you'll get better customers

less churn

more revenue

it's that simple

Claude Code + Cursor

is like having a senior dev 24/7

that never gets tired

and never complains

but YOU have to be the tech lead

that's the catch
